critical_findings.title: Sandbox-first as written = building the entire product on INVENTED adapter contracts; the deferred Phase 0 spike is the highest-risk assumption
critical_findings.argument: SPEC §11 made a real end-to-end spike the explicit de-risk gate ('prove end-to-end before building product around it'). ROADMAP replaces it with sandbox adapters 'coded against public API docs' (B3) and defers ALL real vendor contact to activation. Public API docs for Inboxkit/Porkbun are routinely wrong on the exact things that shape your schema: async provisioning + webhooks, error shapes, rate limits, field names, DNS/DKIM propagation timing, and the per-tenant isolation model (§16.1 is UNCONFIRMED). The sandbox is a fiction the builder invents. You will bake those invented contracts into Durable Object schemas, the provisioning job state machine, and the facade intents, THEN discover at activation that the real APIs behave differently — after the whole platform, AI-ops, and distribution are built around the wrong shape. This is exactly the 'should have been caught' build-drag the plan was supposed to prevent, now guaranteed at the worst possible moment. The single highest-risk assumption of the whole plan is: 'a product can be built and hardened to done against sandbox adapters, deferring all real vendor contact to activation' — when the adapter contracts are precisely what sandbox cannot verify.
critical_findings.fix: Insert a Phase A step 'A0.5 — zero-spend real-contract capture': issue FREE API keys (Inboxkit, Porkbun), hit the real endpoints read-only, and capture actual request/response/error/async/isolation schemas. Build the sandbox adapters as REPLAYS of captured real fixtures, not invented shapes. Use Stripe TEST mode (the real Stripe API, free) rather than a Stripe fiction. This kills the risk while fully honoring the locked 'no PAID provisioning' constraint — the lock is on spend, not on API contact.

critical_findings.title: The business model itself (resell wholesale at markup) is unvalidated and could be ToS-prohibited or unprofitable — discovered only at activation
critical_findings.argument: The entire L1 thesis is buy-wholesale/resell-at-2.5-3x under card-on-file multi-tenancy. SPEC §16.2 flags as UNCONFIRMED whether Inboxkit even offers a reseller/agency tier — and worse, whether their ToS PERMITS reselling their mailboxes to your own paying customers under a wrapper. Many infra vendors forbid sub-reselling or gate it behind a sales-call reseller agreement (the very thing §13 tries to avoid). Smartlead has a white-label 'Create Client' API precisely because it's built to be resold; it is unknown whether Inboxkit is. Failure scenario: you build the entire multi-tenant markup-billing platform, then at activation learn either (a) retail-only pricing makes 2.5x underwater once burn+Stripe+support load in, or (b) reselling is contractually prohibited, invalidating the multi-tenant model outright, or (c) the vendor requires per-tenant KYC on THEIR side, breaking the 'one signup + token' friction-collapse that is the whole distribution edge.
critical_findings.fix: Promote §16.1 (per-tenant isolation) + §16.2 (reseller tier + ToS permission + margin) to a Phase A HARD GATE with an explicit NO-GO branch. Both are confirmable at $0: hit the free API to verify programmatic per-tenant isolation and token scoping, and READ the actual vendor ToS for resale permission. If reselling is prohibited/uneconomical, re-pick the vendor (Mailreef/Maildoso) or restructure the legal model BEFORE building — not after.

critical_findings.title: The single most critical component (Go send/reply engine) can't run on the Cloudflare-first architecture; its REAL path is never exercised and it's a rearchitecture bomb at activation
critical_findings.argument: ROADMAP line 49 admits the forked cold-cli (long-lived IMAP/SMTP) 'cannot run on Workers' and defers the Go fork + container host as 'a real-adapter concern at activation,' substituting a 'native sandbox implementation in the Worker.' But the sequencing/reply engine is the product's hands — the thing that actually sends and reads email. So the whole platform is architected on Workers/DO/D1 while the one component that does the core job lives nowhere, is faked in-Worker the entire build, and its real path (the Go binary + its own SQLite/Postgres + DO↔engine state sync) is exercised for the first time at activation. A solo autonomous agent will stall hard here: choosing a container host, wiring state sync, and reconciling the fake send-path semantics with the real ones — all at the moment you can least afford it. Compounding: A3 (cold-cli license) is treated as a checkbox, but if the license is non-permissive/absent (§14 warns to confirm), the 'fork = free, MED effort' assumption for B4 collapses to 'build from scratch = HIGH effort,' blowing the timeline for the biggest component.
critical_findings.fix: Decide the Go-engine host NOW as an architecture decision (Cloudflare Containers / a small VPS / Fly) and design the DO↔engine state contract explicitly. Run the forked cold-cli LOCALLY against a personal/test mailbox ($0) to exercise the real send/reply/IMAP path before B4 — do not let 'native sandbox in Worker' stand in for ever running the real engine. Make A3 a gate with a written fallback branch (scratch-build effort estimate) if the license is NO-GO.

critical_findings.title: Definition-of-done is a boil-the-ocean, all-or-nothing trap that front-loads un-sandbox-testable AI-ops hardening
critical_findings.argument: The DoD bundles product + all distribution surfaces + AI-ops lanes + legal + full adversarial hardening + ACTIVATION.md as one monolithic finish line with no internal kill-gate or shippable intermediate. A solo agent grinds for weeks with no point where a human can course-correct against real signal. Worse, D1/D2/B6 build autonomous support triage, deliverability control loops, and ops digests for a business with ZERO real traffic — and the deliverability loop reacts to REAL Gmail/MS signals that don't exist in sandbox (SPEC §10: 'it cannot change how Gmail/Microsoft judge you'). So the plan hardens synthetic behavior that proves nothing about production, while §0.7 + E1 authorize STANDING multi-lens opus panels 'against every part.' Given the owner's own hard token-budget-discipline lesson after a 4M-token run, this risks burning the autonomous build's budget polishing a fiction.
critical_findings.fix: Re-scope DoD into 'test-mode CORE complete' (pipe + control plane + billing + agent surface + minimal guardrails + legal + a publish-now distribution shell + ACTIVATION.md) as the actual finish line, and move un-sandbox-testable tuning (deliverability loop, warmup, deep AI-ops) to a 'build-to-contract, tune-at-activation' backlog. Add a hardening-budget rule: adversarial panels/class-sweeps apply to what's REAL (control plane, auth, tenancy, billing, isolation LOGIC, legal); deliverability/warmup/AI-ops get contract-level review only until activation.

critical_findings.title: Multiple load-bearing lanes for a COMPLETE self-running business are entirely missing from the ROADMAP
critical_findings.argument: The DoD claims 'the entire business runs itself,' but the money-and-continuity lanes that a card-on-file SaaS cannot live without are absent. Card-on-file WILL fail (expiry/insufficient funds) with no dunning flow → silent revenue loss while tenants keep consuming wholesale infra you pay for. Voluntary churn has no teardown/reclaim path AND no answer to who eats the wholesale bill — domains are ANNUAL, so a churn in month 2 eats 10 months of a domain you can't reclaim (a unit-economics hole, not just ops). Abuse offboarding: §0.8 names an 'abuse-drop ToS' but there is NO ops lane to execute a drop (terminate tenant, reclaim infra, honor suppression obligations, handle the money). Cold-email SaaS is a HIGH-chargeback category; a dispute wave can freeze the Stripe account (existential) and there's no refund/dispute lane. There's no owner business-health metrics substrate (MRR, active tenants, margin/tenant, provisioning-failure rate) behind the promised 'digest,' and no backups/DR or master-key management for the D1 tenant↔vendor ledger + per-tenant encrypted creds (lose it = you can't manage anyone's infra).
critical_findings.fix: Add ROADMAP lanes before calling it complete: (1) dunning/failed-payment/involuntary-churn; (2) cancellation + infra teardown/reclaim + wholesale-liability-during-notice; (3) abuse-offboarding OPS to execute a ToS drop; (4) refund/chargeback/dispute handling with Stripe-freeze mitigation; (5) owner business-health metrics substrate; (6) backups/DR + retention for the ledger; (7) master-key storage/rotation/recovery; (8) per-tenant SPEND/provisioning caps (distinct from usage quotas — a looping agent could rack up real domain/mailbox purchases at activation).

critical_findings.title: Unit economics unvalidated: 2.5-3x markup may not clear fully-loaded cost once 8-18%/mo domain burn is included
critical_findings.argument: SPEC §7 states domain burn of 8–18% PER MONTH is normal and auto-replace is a built-in feature — meaning you re-buy a meaningful fraction of every tenant's domains monthly. But §12's margin math (retail − wholesale, 2.5–3x) shows none of that recurring replacement cost, nor Stripe fees (2.9%+30c), nor annual-domain amortization against monthly billing, nor support. For a heavy-send tenant (more mailboxes → more domains → more burn), the fully-loaded cost could exceed a flat 2.5–3x markup, making margin negative on exactly your most-active (most-valuable-looking) customers. Pricing authority was delegated (§0.5), but delegating authority doesn't excuse an unmodeled cost base.
critical_findings.fix: Model fully-loaded per-tenant economics ON PAPER now ($0): wholesale mailbox + domain amortization + 8-18%/mo burn-replacement + Stripe fees + support, across light/medium/heavy send profiles. Verify 2.5-3x clears it for heavy tenants or switch to a value metric that scales with burn (per-mailbox + per-domain-replacement passthrough). Set per-tenant spend caps from this model.

critical_findings.title: Distribution is BUILT but never VALIDATED, and the aging-clock surfaces are mis-sequenced behind product
critical_findings.argument: Product-before-distribution build order is correct (agents can't recommend a product that doesn't exist, and §17 rightly treats distribution as a built surface). But two sequencing errors: (1) The plan builds all of Phase C (SEO/AEO, comparison pages, registries) on faith — §17's discovery test is n=1 and there is NO repeatable measurement that any of it actually flips a fresh agent's recommendation. You could ship all of Phase C, publish, and move nothing, and not know. (2) §0.6 LOCKS 'publish public surfaces NOW / aging clock starts immediately,' but the ROADMAP orders Phase C strictly AFTER Phase B — so a serial autonomous agent starts the one thing that can't be compressed later (SEO/AEO indexing latency) weeks late, contradicting the locked decision.
critical_findings.fix: (1) Add a distribution VALIDATION harness: an automated, re-runnable 'fresh coding agent + query X → does it discover/recommend us?' test against your OWNED surfaces (owned-surfaces-only, respects §0.9), baselined against §17's n=1 result and re-run before/after each Phase C surface ships. (2) Split Phase C: publish-now shell surfaces (repo, waitlist page, llms.txt, docs skeleton) run in PARALLEL with Phase B to start the aging clock immediately per §0.6; deep content (comparison pages) lands after the product is describable.

critical_findings.title: 'Test-mode complete' certifies everything EXCEPT the only thing that determines customer value (deliverability)
critical_findings.argument: SPEC's own honesty admits the core value — do emails land in inboxes — depends on real Gmail/MS judgment (§10) and that warmup shows 'no meaningful lift' in independent tests (§9). Sandbox-first, by construction, cannot test this: there are no real deliverability signals to react to. So 'test-mode complete + adversarially hardened' can be declared while the single thing that makes a customer succeed is entirely unproven. If you flip live and onboard a paying customer, that customer's campaign IS your first deliverability experiment — a brutal first impression in a category where reputation compounds.
critical_findings.fix: State plainly in the DoD that 'test-mode complete' = 'plumbing proven, VALUE unproven.' Make ACTIVATION.md's FIRST post-activation gate the deferred Phase 0 real-world spike (buy 1 domain → DNS → 2 mailboxes → send → confirm inbox placement → detect reply), executed and passing BEFORE any paying customer is onboarded — so the deferral of Phase 0 becomes a scheduled gate, not a silent hole.

missing_items: Voluntary cancellation + infra teardown/reclaim + wholesale-liability-during-notice (annual domains)
Abuse-offboarding OPS to execute the abuse-drop ToS
Refund / chargeback / dispute handling (Stripe-freeze risk on cold-email SaaS)
Owner business-health metrics substrate (MRR, active tenants, margin/tenant, provisioning-failure, incident rate)
Backups / DR / retention for the D1 tenant↔vendor ledger + encrypted creds
Master-key storage / rotation / recovery for per-tenant encrypted vendor credentials
Per-tenant spend/provisioning caps distinct from usage quotas (cost-runaway guard)
Our own transactional-email deliverability, ideally on a separate reputation
Status page / incident comms for a runs-itself product
amendments: Add Phase A step A0.5 'zero-spend real-contract capture': issue FREE Inboxkit/Porkbun API keys, capture real request/response/error/async/isolation schemas, and build sandbox adapters as REPLAYS of captured real fixtures (not invented shapes). Use Stripe TEST mode (the real API) not a fiction. Honors the no-PAID-spend lock while killing the sandbox-fiction risk.

Promote SPEC §16.1 (per-tenant isolation) + §16.2 (reseller tier + ToS resale-permission + margin) to a Phase A HARD GATE with an explicit NO-GO branch; confirm via free API + reading the actual vendor ToS. If resale is prohibited/uneconomical, re-pick vendor or restructure BEFORE building.

Decide the Go-engine container host NOW and design the DO↔engine state-sync contract; run the forked cold-cli LOCALLY against a test mailbox ($0) to exercise the real send/reply/IMAP path before B4. Do not let 'native sandbox in Worker' substitute for running the real engine.
Make A3 (cold-cli license) a gate with a written NO-GO fallback: if the license is non-permissive/absent, re-estimate B4 as a from-scratch build (HIGH effort) and adjust the plan before proceeding.

Re-scope the DoD into 'test-mode CORE complete' (pipe + control plane + billing + agent surface + minimal guardrails + legal + publish-now distribution shell + ACTIVATION.md) as the finish line, and move un-sandbox-testable tuning (deliverability loop, warmup, deep AI-ops, standing panels) to a 'tune-at-activation' backlog with contract-level review only.

Split Phase C: publish-now shell surfaces (repo, waitlist, llms.txt, docs skeleton) run in PARALLEL with Phase B to start the SEO/AEO aging clock immediately per locked §0.6; deep comparison content lands after the product is describable.

Add a re-runnable distribution VALIDATION harness ('fresh agent + query → does it discover/recommend us?') against OWNED surfaces only, baselined on §17's n=1 result, run before/after each Phase C surface ships.

Model fully-loaded per-tenant unit economics on paper now ($0): wholesale + 8-18%/mo domain-burn replacement + Stripe fees + domain amortization + support, across light/medium/heavy send profiles; confirm 2.5-3x clears it or change the value metric.

ACTIVATION.md's FIRST post-activation gate = the deferred Phase 0 real-world deliverability smoke test (buy 1 domain → DNS → 2 mailboxes → send → confirm inbox placement → detect reply), passing BEFORE onboarding any paying customer.

Add a hardening-budget rule: adversarial panels/class-sweeps apply to what is REAL (control plane, auth, tenancy, billing, isolation logic, legal); deliverability/warmup/AI-ops get contract-level review only until activation — protects against burning the autonomous budget polishing synthetic behavior.
